title: Red Hat Security Advisory: libsoup3 security update
overview:
An update for libsoup3 is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.0 Extended Update Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: libsoup3 security update

An update for libsoup3 is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.0 Extended Update Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for libsoup3 is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.0 Extended Update Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

Updated Packages:
libsoup3-3.6.5-3.el10_0.11.src.rpm
libsoup3-3.6.5-3.el10_0.11.x86_64.rpm
libsoup3-debuginfo-3.6.5-3.el10_0.11.x86_64.rpm
libsoup3-debugsource-3.6.5-3.el10_0.11.x86_64.rpm
libsoup3-devel-3.6.5-3.el10_0.11.x86_64.rpm
libsoup3-3.6.5-3.el10_0.11.s390x.rpm
libsoup3-debuginfo-3.6.5-3.el10_0.11.s390x.rpm
libsoup3-debugsource-3.6.5-3.el10_0.11.s390x.rpm
libsoup3-devel-3.6.5-3.el10_0.11.s390x.rpm
libsoup3-3.6.5-3.el10_0.11.ppc64le.rpm
libsoup3-debuginfo-3.6.5-3.el10_0.11.ppc64le.rpm
libsoup3-debugsource-3.6.5-3.el10_0.11.ppc64le.rpm
libsoup3-devel-3.6.5-3.el10_0.11.ppc64le.rpm
libsoup3-3.6.5-3.el10_0.11.aarch64.rpm
libsoup3-debuginfo-3.6.5-3.el10_0.11.aarch64.rpm
libsoup3-debugsource-3.6.5-3.el10_0.11.aarch64.rpm
libsoup3-devel-3.6.5-3.el10_0.11.aarch64.rpm
libsoup3-doc-3.6.5-3.el10_0.11.noarch.rpm

CVE-2025-14523: A flaw in libsoup’s HTTP header handling allows multiple Host: headers in a request and returns the last occurrence for server-side processing. Common front proxies often honor the first Host: header, so this mismatch can cause vhost confusion where a proxy routes a request to one backend but the backend interprets it as destined for another host. This discrepancy enables request-smuggling style attacks, cache poisoning, or bypassing host-based access controls when an attacker supplies duplicate Host headers.
